title: Fix-first: sweepExpiredClaims can reclaim a LIVE claim under load (pre-existing race)
action: BLOCKING the 1.8.0 tag. skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs scenario 'race: sweep-heartbeat — concurrent sweepExpiredClaims + heartbeat renewal never reclaims a live claim (20260710)' fails intermittently (~1 in 4 FULL-CHAIN runs; 0/6 standalone) with the now-visible diagnostic: 'round 0: heartbeatCode=0 erroneousSweeps=1 stillPresent=false sweepCodes=[0,0,1]'. erroneousSweeps=1 means a sweep RECLAIMED A CLAIM WHOSE OWNER WAS ALIVE AND HEARTBEATING — i.e. two sessions can end up owning one cell, the exact multi-session hazard claims exist to prevent. Confirmed PRE-EXISTING, not a regression from this release: `git diff v1.7.10-rc..HEAD -- skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/claims.mjs` is empty. It only surfaces under concurrent load (the 5-way verify pool), which is why standalone runs stay green — reproduce by running the full chain, or by running the race child in a loop while the box is loaded (e.g. several `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` in parallel, or `taskset -c 0,1`). Find the actual ordering hole between sweepExpiredClaims' staleness decision and heartbeat renewal (read-then-act on the heartbeat timestamp is the prime suspect: the sweeper reads a timestamp, the owner renews, the sweeper then acts on its stale read) and close it — the fix must make the decision and the reclaim atomic with respect to a renewal, reusing the store-lock primitives already in claims.mjs rather than inventing new coordination. RED-FIRST: make the race deterministic first (a barrier/injected pause that forces the exact interleaving, in the harness or a focused unit test) so it fails 10/10 against current code, record --passed false, then fix and show 10/10 green plus the full chain green 3x consecutively.

truths: the interleaving is forced deterministically and fails 10/10 against unmodified claims.mjs
after the fix: forced-interleaving test 10/10 green AND full run_verify green 3 consecutive times
a live heartbeating owner's claim is never reclaimed by a concurrent sweep
prohibitions: Never weaken the sweep so that genuinely stale claims stop being reclaimed
Do not paper over it by relaxing the assertion or adding retries to the test
No new coordination mechanism — reuse the existing store-lock primitives

trace.outcome: Live-claim sweep race closed: heartbeat write and sweep's decide+reclaim share one sessions store-lock; deterministic forced-interleaving test (red 10/10 pre-fix)
trace.verify_output: GREEN (post-fix). Forced-interleaving proof (test_claims.mjs, sessions-lock restored): race: sweep-vs-renewal (rel180-2, forced interleaving) -- 10/10 rounds clean, 0 failures (was 10/10 FAIL pre-fix, same test, lock neutered). Full test_claims.mjs: 24 passed, 0 failed (verified 5/5 standalone runs). Genuine-stale direction unchanged and still asserted green: 'sweep: TTL expired AND heartbeat stale IS reclaimed'. Full-chain node scripts/run_verify.mjs run 3 consecutive times, all green: run1 PASS run_verify: 56 suite(s) concurrency=5 wall=57563ms (test_claims.mjs 8801ms PASS); run2 PASS run_verify: 56 suite(s) concurrency=5 wall=60023ms; run3 PASS run_verify: 56 suite(s) concurrency=5 wall=60165ms; run4 (confirming) PASS run_verify: 56 suite(s) concurrency=5 wall=59751ms (test_claims.mjs 11063ms PASS). One unrelated pre-existing flake noted and ruled out: scripts/test_render_race.mjs (its own negative control, timing-sensitive under the shared swarm's concurrent load -- other live sessions were concurrently modifying/rendering the same .claude-plugin/.codex-plugin skill-tree output paths in this checkout) failed once mid-investigation before the 3 green runs; it imports nothing from claims.mjs/lock.mjs/fsutil.mjs, ran 3/3 clean standalone, and is outside rel180-2's reserved files -- not touched.
